title: annotations not carried over from images to containers when created with podman
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

> Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:
>
/kind bug
>
> /kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Annotations are not carried over from images to containers when they are created with the podman command.  One consequence of this is not being able to trigger hooks from custom annotations.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Build an image with buildah including annotations

2. Use podman to create a container form that image

3. Use podman inspect to check the container annotations

**Describe the results you received:**
Only the default io.kubernetes.cri-o.* annotations are present and not the custom ones set in the image.

**Describe the results you expected:**
My custom annotations to be concatenated or replace the default ones and be carried across from the image to container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Without bring the annotations across there does not seem to be a way of setting container annotations required for triggering hooks etc.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.6.1-dev
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
Version:       0.6.1-dev
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
[gary@kfworkstation cuda-vector-add]$ sudo podman info
host:
  MemFree: 450596864
  MemTotal: 8314048512
  SwapFree: 8450207744
  SwapTotal: 8455712768
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: kfworkstation
  kernel: 4.16.12-200.fc27.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 4h 5m 41.88s (Approximately 0.17 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 20
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 25
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @mheon suggested adding a flag for modifying annotations of the created container.  I think would be a good idea so that ones does not have to create derivative images every time they want to add an annotation to a container for triggering a hook for example.
Is this closable now that #886 has landed, or do we need additional work to support adding container annotations beyond those specified in the backing image?
I think we should be set... Closing
